tweets.title: Nobody knows how many bitcoin nodes exist. The listening ones can be counted, but the silent ones can’t, by design. There could be millions and millions of computers running the Bitcoin software with a fully audited copy of the blockchain protecting it and we have no idea.
Tonight, while you sleep, the world will agree approximately 36 more times about the Bitcoin blockchain. Nobody will mention the incredible achievement on the news in the morning.

Nobody can say what Satoshi Nakamoto’s million bitcoin fortune is doing. Not “saved”, because no one is saving them. Not “spent”, they have never moved. Not “lost”, the key may exist. A thing we can observe perfectly and cannot name? “Donated” is as close as we can describe it.

In 1981, a gigabyte of storage cost more than a house. Today the complete financial history of bitcoin, every transaction on the blockchain since the first, fits on a drive that costs less than dinner.
